items.q: How do SSRIs help with OCD?
items.correct: They prevent reabsorption of serotonin, increasing its availability in the synapse.
items.misconception: They directly produce more serotonin in the brain.
items.why: Students think SSRIs create serotonin. They actually inhibit REUPTAKE - serotonin already released stays in the synapse longer, increasing post-synaptic stimulation.

items.q: What is the difference between reliability and validity?
items.correct: Reliability is consistency; validity is whether it measures what it claims to.
items.misconception: They are essentially the same thing.
items.random_wrong: Reliability is for quantitative, validity for qualitative.
items.why: Students treat these as synonyms. A bathroom scale that adds 5kg every time is reliable (consistent) but not valid (wrong reading). You can be reliable without being valid.

items.q: How are phobias MAINTAINED according to Mowrer's two-process model?
items.correct: Through operant conditioning - avoidance reduces anxiety (negative reinforcement).
items.misconception: Through classical conditioning continuing over time.
items.why: Classical conditioning ACQUIRES the phobia; operant conditioning MAINTAINS it. Students conflate these. Avoiding the feared object reduces anxiety, reinforcing the avoidance.

items.q: Adding a single dissenter dropped conformity to about:
items.why: Correct answer: 5%. “25%” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Conformity rose more when the task was:
items.why: Correct answer: Harder. “Easier” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: After roughly how many confederates does conformity plateau in Asch?
items.why: The real figure is 3. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
